what_it_does: Defines a manual 'launch-surfaces' routine that reports Tim's two review-surface URLs and their up/down status by curling local ports, and flags a needed restart without performing it.
observations: The file is a routine definition (a Python dict named ROUTINE) rather than executable logic.
It checks http://localhost:8090/ for the gallery and http://localhost:5174/ for the live surface using curl read-only status codes.
It reports external URLs https://workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net and https://workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net:8443 with up/down labels.
Restarting the gallery requires running /home/tim/repos/counterpart/design/engine/serve.sh, but the routine explicitly refuses to auto-restart; a person/agent must confirm.
It is triggered manually via the routines MCP tool op=fire or routine_runner.fire('launch-surfaces').
Permission mode is default, cadence manual, repeats false, max_turns 4.
standouts: Strong safety framing: approval gates stay on and auto-restart is explicitly disclaimed.
The routine's real output is two hard-coded Tailscale URLs, with local curl probes only determining up/down.
It is essentially a prompt-backed registry entry, not code that performs the checks itself.
conventions: Uses a top-level ROUTINE dict with id/label/description/prompt/cwd/permission_mode/cadence/repeats/max_turns/trigger fields.
Includes a docstring summarizing scope, safety guard, and invocation path.
Hard-codes hostnames and ports rather than reading from configuration.
Mixes operational commands (curl) inside a natural-language prompt instead of executable code.
concerns: The prompt embeds exact shell commands and expected reply format, so behavior depends on the consumer's ability to parse/execute the prompt reliably.
URLs and ports are hard-coded, making the routine brittle if endpoints move.
The 'up/down' determination is a single HTTP status check with no timeout, retry, or TLS/health nuance specified.
The reply format demands exact strings but is not enforced by code in this file.
notes: A manual, read-only status routine for Tim's review surfaces that tells you if the gallery or phone surface is down and asks a human/agent to restart the gallery if needed.
questions: Which agent/runtime interprets the prompt and executes the curl commands?
What do 'DNA gallery' and 'live-surface phone' refer to, and are there other surfaces?
Is 'permission_mode': 'default' sufficient for a routine that can request a restart script execution?
How is the Tailscale hostname kept in sync with the local ports being probed?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: it is a registry-backed reminder/manual status check that returns URLs and up/down status and does not auto-restart.
apparent_themes: operational safety through explicit human/agent confirmation
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn a frequently-needed 'are my review surfaces up?' check into a one-shot, discoverable routine that surfaces actionable next steps while refusing to bypass approval gates.
novelty: It encodes a safe-launching policy as a routine definition: the refusal to auto-restart with disabled approvals is part of the artifact itself, not a runtime guard.
contribution: Provides a reusable, named manual routine that documents how to check Tim's review surfaces and the correct restart path, keeping the approval gate decision explicit and outside the routine.
summary_for_embedding: A manual routine definition that checks local gallery and surface ports and reports Tim's two review-surface Tailscale URLs as up or down, flagging gallery restart as a gated human/agent action.
intention_for_embedding: To make launching/status-checking of Tim's review surfaces a remembered, registry-backed routine that never disables approval gates or auto-restarts.
